CFDocument.creator: Georgia Department of Education
CFDocument.title: CTAE - Georgia Standards of Excellence: Career Cluster: Marketing

CFItems.fullStatement: Marketing Principles (MKT–MP) *
Demonstrate employability skills required by business and industry. *
CFItems.notes: The following standard is included in all CTAE courses adopted for the Career Cluster/Pathways. Teachers should incorporate the elements of this standard into lesson plans during the course. The topics listed for each element of the standard may be addressed in differentiated instruction matching the content of each course. These elements may also be addressed with specific lessons from a variety of resources. This content is not to be treated as a unit or separate body of knowledge but rather integrated into class activities as applications of the concept.

CFItems.fullStatement: Communicate effectively through writing, speaking, listening, reading, and interpersonal abilities.
Demonstrate creativity by asking challenging questions and applying innovative procedures and methods.
Exhibit critical thinking and problem solving skills to locate, analyze and apply information in career planning and employment situations.
Model work readiness traits required for success in the workplace including integrity, honesty, accountability, punctuality, time management, and respect for diversity.
Apply the appropriate skill sets to be productive in a changing, technological, diverse workplace to be able to work independently and apply team work skills.
Present a professional image through appearance, behavior and language.
Marketing and Entrepreneurship
CFItems.notes:
**Course Description:**
Marketing and Entrepreneurship is the second course in the Marketing and Management Career Pathway. Marketing and Entrepreneurship begins an in-depth and detailed study of marketing
while also focusing on management with specific emphasis on small business ownership. This course builds on the theories learned in Marketing Principles by providing practical application scenarios which test these theories. In addition, Marketing and Entrepreneurship focuses on the role of the supervisor and examines the qualities needed to be successful.

**Document Date:**
October 11, 2013

Compose promotional messages that appeal to targeted markets.
Examine advertising proofs.
Develop concepts for a broadcast advertisement.
Explain the use of direct advertising strategies.
Develop collateral pieces for direct-mail advertising.
Explain the practice of online advertising in the promotion industry.
Describe the use of e-mail marketing in the promotion industry.
Design sales promotion materials.
Design and implement a multi-media branding strategy.
Determine the target audience for a brand.
Develop a brand identity (look and feel).
Identify dimensions of brand personality and voice.
Articulate a brand story.
Communicate the importance of having a business presence on social media platforms.
Research and list best practices in developing content for social media promotion.
Monitor online brand presence and reputation.
Recognize data measurement tools, analytics, and key performance indicators for advertising and digital marketing.
Identify various key performance indicators (KPI) utilized to assess marketing objectives.
Utilize web and social media analytics tools to evaluate a promotion campaign.
Explain how SEO (search engine optimization) works to increase the effectiveness of digital marketing.
Make recommendations how a website can improve organic search rankings.
Describe how a bounce rate is calculated and how it is utilized to measure the effectiveness of a website.
Explain how CPC (cost-per-click), CPP (cost-per-point) and CPM (cost-per-thousand) are calculated and what they represent.
Discuss goals for analytics and identify conversion points.

Investigate and explain the concepts and strategies needed to communicate information about products, services, images, and ideas to achieve a desired outcome using digital marketing communications media.
Explain the nature of online advertising (e.g., social media, online gaming, display ads, pop- up ads, pay-per-click ads).
Explain the nature of targeted emails.
Explain the role of business websites in digital marketing.
Explain the use of video and images for digital marketing.
Discuss the role of audio recordings such as podcasts in digital marketing.
Research and explain OTT (over the top) targeted ad delivery.
Identify potential career opportunities in the Marketing Communications and Promotion pathway with appropriate career path credentials.
Identify appropriate individual career titles and career paths within the Marketing Communications & Promotion pathway.
Determine the education, skills, and training needed to enter a selected career in the Marketing Communications & Promotion pathway.
Conduct a self-assessment of the Marketing Communications & Promotion pathway skill set.
Create a career plan to obtain the education, skills, and training needed to enter a selected sales or promotion career.
Explain factors affecting the growth and development of the marketing communications industry including a global perspective.
Identify requirements for professional certifications in the Marketing Communications and Promotion career pathway.
Assess the services of professional organizations in the Marketing Communications & Promotion pathway.
Examine how related student organizations are integral parts of career and technology education courses through leadership development, school, and community service projects and competitive events.
Explain the purpose, mission, objectives, motto, colors, official dress, and other distinguishing characteristics of DECA.
Explain how participation in DECA can promote lifelong responsibility for community service, professional growth, and development.

Explore the impact and opportunities DECA can develop to bring business and industry together with education in a positive working relationship through innovative leadership and career development programs.

Explore the local, state, and national opportunities available to students through participation in DECA including but not limited to conferences, competitions, community service, philanthropy, and other DECA activities.
Integrated Marketing Communications (MKT-IMC)
Demonstrate employability skills required by business and industry.
Communicate effectively through writing, speaking, listening, reading, and interpersonal abilities.
Demonstrate creativity by asking challenging questions and applying innovative procedures and methods.
Model work readiness traits required for success in the workplace including integrity, honesty, accountability, punctuality, time management, and respect for diversity.
Apply the appropriate skill sets to be productive in a changing, technological, diverse workplace to be able to work independently and apply team work skills.
Present a professional image through appearance, behavior and language.
Formulate and apply the concepts and processes needed to obtain, develop, maintain, and improve a product or service mix in response to market opportunities.
Explain business ethics in product/service management.
Identify consumer protection provisions of appropriate agencies.
Describe the use of technology in the product/service management function.
Describe services offered by the marketing-communications industry.
Generate marketing communications ideas.
Explain the nature of product extension in services marketing.
Recommend product offerings based on current trends.
Distinguish the tools, techniques, and systems that businesses use to analyze the effectiveness of marketing communications.
Describe current issues and trends in marketing communications.
Obtain a basic understanding of analytics.
Recognize social media analytics and navigate the tools available.
Identify which metrics are important to track for social media marketing.
Describe what is comprised in a social media analytics plan.
Explain the importance and the steps of a social media audit.
Conduct a social media audit.
Develop a logical argument about the concepts and strategies needed to communicate information about products, services, images, and ideas to achieve a desired outcome.
Explain the marketing communications development process.
Explain the use of product placement in media.
Repurpose content for use in multiple platforms.
Identify benefits of diversifying media.
Identify techniques to increase ad response time.
Explain reach and frequency and the concept of impression- based marketing.
Discuss the concepts of social engagement.
Describe referral programs that can be used to build brands and promote products.
Describe considerations in developing brand ambassadors.
Create and implement a fully integrated marketing campaign.
Conduct a SWOT Analysis of a business to prepare for an integrated marketing campaign (IMC).
Describe the importance of integrated marketing communications.
Compose objectives for an integrated marketing campaign (IMC).
Integrate and plan marketing communications efforts.
Evaluate budget considerations for a marketing communications plan.
Identify the key performance indicators (KPI) and explain how to monitor an IMC.
Investigate the tools, strategies, and systems needed to access, process, maintain, evaluate, and disseminate information to assist marketing information decision-making.
Discuss the nature and types of sampling plans (e.g., who, how many, how chosen).
Compare and contrast different types of rating scales (e.g., Likert scales, semantic differential scales, behavior intention scales).
Evaluate questionnaire design (e.g., types of questions, question wording, routing, sequencing, length, and layout).
Conduct a market analysis (e.g., market size, area, potential).
Develop and administer a market research survey.
Analyze and present the results of a market research survey.
Monitor competitor’s promotional efforts.
Track and list current trends in industry.
Analyze the concepts and strategies utilized in determining and adjusting prices to maximize return and meet customers’ perceptions of value.
Describe the role of business ethics in pricing.
Discuss the role of various pricing strategies for promotions.
Research competitor’s pricing.
Explain the use of technology in the pricing function.
Develop pricing models for products offered.
Determine profit margins.

Summarize the concepts and actions needed to determine client needs and wants and respond through planned, personalized communication that influences purchase decisions and enhances future business opportunities.
Acquire knowledge of a potential client’s products and brands.
Determine needs and wants of a client for a promotional campaign.
Calculate a budget and range of costs for a promotional campaign.
Develop a formal presentation of a promotion campaign for a business client.
Utilize persuasion tactics to promote ideas for marketing communications.
Present a marketing communications plan to a client.
Demonstrate use of audiovisual aides.
Differentiate the concepts and strategies needed to communicate information about products, services, images, and/or ideas to achieve a desired outcome using social communications media.
Compare and contrast various platforms for digital marketing.
Identify methods and sources available to measure effectiveness of digital marketing and social media.
Discuss considerations in using mobile technology for promotional activities including comparing the capabilities of Short Message Service (SMS) with Multi-media Message Service (MMS).
Describe the use of blogging for marketing communications.
Explain the use of Really Simple Syndication (RSS) feeds.
Describe mobile marketing tactics (e.g., geo-fencing).
Develop content for use in social media including formatting and placement sizing.
Explain how to effectively incorporate video into multimedia.
Explain considerations in developing viral marketing campaigns.
Identify strategies for attracting targeted audience to website.
Identify ways to provide value to an audience on social media.
Identify organic engagement tactics to build relationships with an audience on social media.
Utilize a creative brief to plan content for social media posts.
Develop a content calendar to strategize and plan for consistency in a campaign.
Implement a digital marketing campaign.
Identify and interpret the importance of selling to the economy.
Explain how selling is an integral part of every major industry.
Distinguish between wholesale, retail, and professional sales (e.g., business to business selling, use of agents, use of manufacturing representatives).
Discuss traits of a successful salesperson.
Explore methods of incentives and compensation.
Explain the nature of sales forecasts.
Describe the use of technology, ecommerce, and digital marketing in the selling function.
Explain the amount of time required to sell a product (e.g., sales cycles).
Examine the increased importance of ecommerce.
Explain how consumer behavior affects promotion and marketing communications.
Describe and provide examples of the consumer motivation process.
Explain types of consumer buying behaviors.
Identify influences on consumer buying behavior.
Examine applied advertising appeals.
Evaluate how social media and other technologies have impacted communications and consumer behaviors.
Explain the role of the conversion funnel in marketing.
Examine how digital promotions and mobile apps affect consumer behaviors.
Discuss how geofencing influences target marketing and consumer behavior.
Analyze and apply the steps needed for an effective sales presentation.
Obtain customer and product knowledge needed for an effective presentation.
Incorporate prospecting techniques to develop a customer base.
Develop cold-calling skills to initiate customer contact.
Develop questioning techniques to identify customer needs and wants.
Identify and resolve customer questions and objections.
Demonstrate methods for closing the sale.
Articulate the importance associated with suggestive selling techniques.
Use feature-benefit selling for an effective sales presentation.
Utilize appropriate techniques when opening a sales presentation.
Explain ways to create effective virtual sales presentations.
Describe the importance of utilizing follow-up techniques after the sale has been completed.
Describe the importance of utilizing personal notes concerning sales follow-up.
Demonstrate an appropriate follow-up phone call.
Articulate the importance of obtaining positive customer referrals for future sales.
Identify voice of the customer (VOC) regarding sales process.
Illustrate ways to follow-up a virtual purchase.
Describe Customer Relationship Management (CRM) including loyalty and retention marketing.
Apply the concepts and strategies needed to communicate information about products, services, images, and ideas to achieve a desired outcome using sales promotions.
Explain how businesses can use tradeshow/exposition participation to communicate with targeted audiences.
Explain how sales promotions are used in direct response marketing.
Collaborate in the design of collateral materials to promote frequency/loyalty marketing program.
Participate in the design of collateral materials to promote a special event.
Set up cross-promotions.
Plan and create visual displays.
Create promotional signage.
Examine how related student organizations are integral parts of career and technology education courses through leadership development, school, and community service projects and competitive events.
Explain the purpose, mission, objectives, motto, colors, official dress, and other distinguishing characteristics of DECA.
Explain how participation in DECA can promote lifelong responsibility for community service, professional growth, and development.

Explore the impact and opportunities DECA can develop to bring business and industry together with education in a positive working relationship through innovative leadership and career development programs.

Explore the local, state, and national opportunities available to students through participation in DECA including but not limited to conferences, competitions, community service, philanthropy, and other DECA activities.
International Business & Marketing (MKT-IBM)
Students will develop an understanding of the fundamental concepts of international business and marketing.
Explain the difference between a domestic and international company.
Report on historical developments illustrating the past experience of the United States’ role in international trade and in the international landscape for business.
Identify international trade partners and point out the trading patterns of companies based on region, state, and country.
Demonstrate an understanding of the relationship between international events and the daily conduct of business.
Determine the impact of geography on international business to include areas such as climate, time zones, distance, topography, and social, economic, and natural resources.
Explain the role of international business at local, regional, and national levels citing examples at each level and how each level affects the world economy.
Analyze the potential impact (on a community, region, state, and country in which it is located) of a domestic company involving itself in international trade opportunities.
Students will analyze various factors and influences affecting the international business environment.

Identify international cultural differences in food, dress, language, gift-giving, social behaviors, and major holidays of various cultures and discuss how they are celebrated as well as their impact on the business community.
Analyze the components of the Foreign Corrupt Practices Act and its impact on a company's activities.

Identify and differentiate between types of governments and political environments and determine the impact of the government on international business through the use of barriers, tariffs, quotas, and taxation policies.
Recognize legal differences that exist between and among countries in areas such as consumer protection, product guidelines, labor laws, contract formulation, liability, and taxation.
Describe the decision-making process, opportunity costs, and scarcity as they relate to international business situations.
Define terms such as GDP (GNP), balance of trade, foreign debt, and cost of living.
Analyze the availability of resources (natural, human, and capital) in a country and the economic potential of the country to improve its quality of life by engaging in international trade.
Students will apply appropriate informational technology and communication strategies for international business and marketing situations.
Determine how to communicate internationally using electronic communications (i.e., telephone, fax, telex) and channels (i.e., Internet, email).
Evaluate electronic communications and channels as appropriate for given international business and marketing situations.
Apply communication strategies (oral, written, and non-verbal) necessary and appropriate for effective and profitable international business and marketing relations.
Analyze complications involved in communication strategies (oral, written, and non-verbal) in an international business and marketing environment.
Students will analyze the importance of ethical business operations in an international business environment.
Identify the levels of regulation applied to intellectual properties (e.g., copyrights, trademarks, and patents) in different countries.
Discuss the Marketing Code of Ethics and the application of the Marketing Code of Ethics in international business.
Discuss the importance of maintaining integrity in dealing with others.
Demonstrate awareness of social responsibilities.
Define terms such as ethics, social responsibility, and bribe.
Identify groups to whom international companies are responsible.
Analyze the components of the Foreign Corrupt Practices Act and its impact on a company's activities.
Analyze the effect of an international business organization's actions on a host country, the company's home country, owners, employees, consumers, and society.
Students will determine characteristics of various organizational structures in the international business environment.
Suggest an appropriate form of business ownership (e.g., sole proprietorship, corporation, partnership) for different international business situations.
Determine social and cultural influences on the form of business ownership used or required in different countries.
Identify companies doing business in a foreign market and identify potential new international business ventures for locally based companies.
Identify risks and rewards related to doing business in a foreign market.
Identify organizations, government agencies, and other resources that a small and/or medium-size business might use to investigate international trade opportunities.
Describe situations appropriate for indirect and direct exporting.
Distinguish between licensing and franchising for international business activities.
Describe internal and external factors which influence a company’s international expansion.
Students will determine the importance of market research, product development and pricing strategies in successful international marketing strategies.
Describe how marketing mix elements need to be adapted for international marketing efforts for goods and services.
Identify characteristics of international consumer markets and commercial markets including social, cultural, and political factors that would affect international marketing.
Explain how the marketing research process, including secondary data and primary data collection, differs in an international application.
Create a data collection survey instrument for an international marketing research study based on knowledge of social and cultural factors in a specific foreign market.
Explain how brands and packages are affected by culture and determine situations in which global (standardized) versus international (adapted) products would be sold.
Describe how the product life cycle differs in the international business environment.
Develop an international marketing plan for a company entering a foreign market.
Students will evaluate effective methods of professional sales and promotional activities in the international business and marketing environment.
Define the promotional mix and provide examples of each aspect of the promotional mix as used in international marketing strategies.
Discuss media used in different foreign markets.
Analyze the influence of social and cultural factors affecting promotions used in foreign markets.
Develop an understanding of major laws and regulations governing advertising in foreign markets.
Create a sales presentation for a product which is appropriate to a foreign market.
Discuss social and cultural factors in various countries that will affect a successful sales presentation.
Evaluate the effectiveness of a promotional campaign used by a company in a foreign market.
Students will analyze channels of distribution and appropriate logistics required to successfully complete an international business transaction.

Contrast direct and indirect distribution channels for international business and marketing by identifying various distribution intermediaries (i.e., agents, wholesalers, retailers, freight forwarders, export companies, trading companies, and customs brokers) and discuss their functions in international business and marketing.
Describe main methods of transporting (i.e., surface, air, water, electronic) used for international business activities and the situation in which a method would be most appropriate.
Identify how products are prepared for international distribution, including packing and documentation.
Identify different shipping documents and define their purpose.
Evaluate trade barriers and trade agreements and their effect on the company, product, and economics involved in a specific international business and marketing transaction.
Analyze security measures that are needed to protect businesses and products in an international business and marketing transaction.
Students will explain the impact of foreign exchange rates, international finance issues and risk management techniques on international business operations.
Develop an understanding of currencies and currency exchange rates from various countries.
Describe how economic conditions, balance of payment situations, and political issues affect currency values.
Describe the international monetary system and different types of international banking organizations within the system including securities markets and financial markets.
Describe the mechanics, terminology, conditions, and terms of letters of credit and other documents.
Identify major country, foreign exchange, and commercial risks associated with international business and marketing activities.
Discuss strategies to minimize overall risk in international business and marketing transactions.

Students will analyze various management theories and concepts in an international business and marketing setting and the effects of the theories and concepts on human resources and overall business operations.
Identify environmental factors that influence the use of a particular organizational structure for global business operations (e.g., functional, product, geographic, or matrix).
Describe how quality control, quality circle, and total quality management relate to an organization’s international business operations.
Discuss the effect of economic and cultural factors on common styles of management found in foreign countries.
Identify the factors that influence the application of managerial styles in different countries.
Compare ethnocentric, polycentric, regiocentric, and geocentric approaches to managing human resources.
Describe how compensation and employee benefits differ in various cultures.
Identify how differences in occupational health and safety standards impact the conduct of business internationally.
Students will identify potential career opportunities in international business and marketing and appropriate career path credentials.
Identify appropriate individual career titles and career paths within international business and marketing.
Determine the skill requirements and educational qualifications to enter a selected international business and marketing career.
Create a career plan to obtain the skills and educational qualifications to enter a selected international business and marketing career.
List business actions that may positively or negatively impact the environment and consumer well-being of a society.
Marketing Research (MKT-MR)
Students will analyze the impact of marketing research in business and industry.
Define marketing research.
Explain the marketing information system concept.
Describe the role and importance of marketing research as a marketing and management decision-making tool.
Explain marketing research as a means for implementing the marketing concept and for developing and implementing a marketing strategy.
Assess when marketing research is needed and when it should not be conducted.
Compare and contrast basic and applied marketing research.
Distinguish among the various categories of marketing research activities.
Students will differentiate between primary and secondary research.
Differentiate between primary and secondary data.
Identify the general examples of exploratory research.
Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of primary data (i.e., credibility, availability, cost in dollars, cost in terms of time).
Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of secondary data (i.e., availability, relevance, accuracy, and sufficiency).
Discuss various internal and external sources of secondary data.
Search and retrieve data from database sources with computers and Internet technology.
Students will categorize stages in the marketing research process.
Explain that explicit problem definition is essential for effective marketing research.
Diagram the steps in the marketing research process.
Implement the marketing research process.
Students will identify demographic information in order to define a target market.
Define demographics.
Discuss the difference between psychographics and demographics.
Discuss the importance of segmentation of demographics.
Identify and interpret sources of demographic data. (i.e., U.S. Census Bureau, DOL, etc.).
Develop a customer profile.
Distinguish between market and target market.
Students will construct sampling designs in the marketing research process.
Identify the issues concerning the identification of the target population and the selection of the sampling frame.
Explain the concept of census versus sample, including types of sampling situations.
Identify methods for determining sample size and explain the concept of sample distribution.
Distinguish between random sampling and systematic (non-sampling) errors.
Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of the various types of probability and non-probability samples.
Identify how to choose an appropriate sample design.
Students will organize and sort data to create a visual representation of information.
Utilize a computer software spreadsheet or SPSS designed for descriptive analysis.
Interpret the findings from basic data analysis.
Students will identify elements of survey techniques in order to Create primary research instruments.
Outline the steps of questionnaire design including preliminary decisions and the five considerations regarding questionnaire content.
Identify the five general issues in question phrasing, types of response formats, and strategies for sequencing questions.
Explain the process of designing data collection strategies.
Identify the differences among ranking, rating, sorting, and making choices to measure attitudes and preferences.
Evaluate and discuss Likert scales, semantic differentials, and other types of attitude scales.
Recognize that decisions about data collection methods and measurement scales influence the format and layout of questionnaires.
Students will plan research design and implement data collection.
Make data usable by organizing and summarizing data into frequency distributions, proportions, and measurements of central tendency.
Describe data collection systems, decision support systems, and the system components of a marketing information system.
Classify marketing research as exploratory research, descriptive research, or causal research.
Identify and select an appropriate survey research design.
Distinguish between scientific observation and casual observation.
Define experimentation and discuss the requirements necessary for making a true experiment.
Identify the types of laboratory experiments and their applications.
Distinguish between internal and external validity.
Distinguish between standard test marketing method, controlled-store and mini-market tests, electronic test markets, and simulated test markets.

Plan and conduct experiments that include the identification of research objectives, research design and collection methods, treatments, control groups, experimental units, random assignments and replication as well as the identification of possible sources of bias and placebo effects.
Students will evaluate organizational and ethical issues of the Marketing research process.
Recognize the degree of marketing research sophistication in various organizations.
Discuss the organizational structure of marketing research in various organizations.
Explain why ethical questions are philosophical questions.
Define societal norms.
Describe the three parties involved in most research situations and determine how the interactions among them may identify a series of ethical questions.
Identify the rights and obligations of the respondent, the researcher, and the client sponsor.
Take each of the three parties’ perspectives and identify selected issues (i.e., deception, privacy, and advocacy research).
Discuss the role of codes of ethics in marketing research.
Students will identify potential career opportunities in marketing research and appropriate career path credentials.
Identify appropriate individual career titles and career paths within marketing research.
Determine the skill requirements and educational qualifications to enter a selected marketing research.
Create a career plan to obtain the skills and educational qualifications to enter a selected marketing research career.
All Courses
Prepare a buying plan and calculate “open-to-buy” on cost and retail for selected products in an identified business.
Exhibit critical thinking and problem solving skills to locate, analyze and apply information in career planning and employment situations.
Exhibit critical thinking and problem-solving skills to locate, analyze and apply information in career planning and employment situations.
Explore the scope of marketing and fundamental marketing concepts
Define marketing.
Explain the marketing concept and the benefits of marketing.
Relate the marketing concept to customers’ needs and wants.
Demonstrate an understanding of trends and developments in marketing.
Describe marketing functions and related activities.
Examine marketing and its importance in a local, national, and global economy.
Explore career opportunities and investigate the job market in the marketing industry.
Examine the nature of marketing plans and the purpose of market planning.
Identify marketing strategies.
Analyze the marketing mix (5 Ps: Product, Price, Promotion, Place, People).
Explain the concepts of market share, market segmentation, and market identification.
Develop a customer profile.
Identify a target market.
Evaluate the role of situational analysis (SWOT, PEST) in the marketing planning process.
Compare and contrast the factors considered for both internal and external analysis.
Outline the elements and objectives of a marketing plan.
Explain the marketing process (planning, implementation, and control).
Compare and contrast mass marketing to niche marketing.
Acquire foundational knowledge of business and financial concepts to understand the scope and impact of business ownership within the economy.
Explain the role of business in society.
Describe types of business activities and methods to start a business.
Define entrepreneurship.
Investigate types of business ownership.
Explain the importance of corporate social responsibility (CSR).
Examine the role of finance in business and marketing.
Identify the types and purposes of credit.
Describe the various financial documents that are maintained and analyzed when making business and marketing decisions.
Demonstrate an understanding of consumer behaviors, the economic environment, and the economic benefits of marketing.
Explain the relationship between marketing and the economy.
Distinguish between economic goods and services.
Identify economic resources.
Determine economic utilities created by business activities.
Distinguish between various economic systems and the effects on what will be produced, how it will be produced, and for whom it will be produced.
Explain how traditional, command, market, and mixed economic systems affect private ownership and the role of government.
Explain why nations engage in international trade, and factors affecting trade such as culture, politics, barriers to trade, currency fluctuations, comparative advantage, etc.
Identify various measurements used to analyze an economy and how they relate to the marketing process (inflation, CPI, PPI, GDP, GNP, etc.)
Explain the principles of supply and demand including elasticity.
Examine the impacts and phases of the business cycle.
Acquire foundational knowledge of marketing information and research to understand the scope of business and marketing decisions.
Describe the need for marketing information management.
Explain the nature of marketing research.
Identify the types of marketing research (advertising, product, market, and sales).
Evaluate qualitative and quantitative data and collection methods.
Compare and contrast primary vs. secondary data.
Describe data-collection methods (e.g., observations, mail, telephone, Internet, discussion/focus groups, interviews, scanners, and social media).
Identify data and metrics that are analyzed to make marketing decisions.
Demonstrate knowledge of research terms: population, sample, bias, error, and validity.
Examine current trends in marketing research, consider the impacts of technology and social media.
Employ processes and techniques to develop, maintain, and improve a product/service mix to optimize market opportunities.
Explain the nature and scope of the product/service management function.
Examine how businesses determine products/services to produce and sell.
Demonstrate methods/techniques to generate a product idea.
Explain the process of positioning products/services.
Explain the concept of product mix and product/service branding.
Identify the elements of product and corporate branding.
Justify branding objectives.
Identify branding strategies (co-branding, brand extension, etc.)
Investigate the functions of packaging and labeling.
Utilize pricing strategies to maximize return and meet customer’s perception of value.
Explain the nature and scope of the pricing function.
Identify market factors that affect pricing decisions.
Calculate markup, markdown, sale prices, and discounts in dollars and percentages.
Explain legal and ethical considerations for pricing.
Identify common pricing strategies.
Explain the concept of consumers’ perceived value and the impact on pricing.
Identify the impact of product life cycles on marketing decisions.
Compare and contrast wholesale vs. retail pricing.
Examine the use of technology in the pricing function.
Utilize promotional knowledge and skills for communicating information to achieve a desired marketing outcome.
Explain the role of promotion as a marketing function.
Identify the elements of the promotional mix.
Describe the types of promotion.

Recognize promotional channels used to communicate with targeted audiences including types of advertising media, social media, digital media, word-of-mouth, direct marketing, sales promotion, visual merchandising, tradeshows, and public relations.
Identify the components of advertisements.
Describe types of public-relations activities.
Explore social media applications as promotional tools and their impact on marketing communications.
Recognize online marketing and promotion techniques (i.e., SEO, SEM, PPC, etc.)
Identify key performance indicators (KPI) and metrics utilized to measure the effectiveness of a promotion (i.e., reach, engagement, conversion, ROI).
Explain the nature and the costs associated with a promotional plan.
Coordinate activities in the promotional plan.
Investigate ethical issues in promotion and advertising.
Employ processes and techniques to sell goods, services, and ideas.
Explain the nature and scope of the selling function and the role of marketing as a complement to selling.
Identify factors and motivational theories that influence consumer buying behavior.
Explain the role of customer service as a component of selling relationships.
Acquire product information for use in selling.
Analyze product information to identify product features and benefits.
Describe the selling process.
Demonstrate sales techniques.
Develop knowledge of channel management and distribution to manage supply-chain activities.
Explain the nature and scope of channel management and the impact on marketing strategies.
Identify channels of distribution.
Describe the use of technology in channel management.
Analyze how distribution affects the cost of products.
Identify the distinguishing characteristics of retailers, wholesalers, agents, and brokers.
Investigate current issues and trends in supply-chain management

Explore how related student organizations are integral parts of career and technology education courses through leadership development, school and community service projects, entrepreneurship development, and competitive events.
Explain the purpose, mission, objectives, motto, colors, official dress, and other distinguishing characteristics of DECA.
Explain how participation in DECA can promote lifelong responsibility for community service, professional growth, and development.

Explore the impact and opportunities DECA can develop to bring business and industry together with education in a positive working relationship through innovative leadership and career development programs.

Explore the local, state, and national opportunities available to students through participation in DECA including but not limited to conferences, competitions, community service, philanthropy, and other DECA activities.
